issue_title: Operator documents issues tracking
issue_body:
This issue tries to track problems in operator documents. Please feel free to add or comments below if you find other operator documents that confuse you, lack details or does not make sense. You are also very welcome to contribute to the documentation. See also #3504 and #3513 for some guidelines of documentations.
### Activation

Ref: #3538
- [x] Softmax activation? The doc says _Softmax Activation is only available with CUDNN on GPUand will be computed at each location across channel if input is 4D._ but the `act_type` does not contain Softmax.
- [x] `softrelu`: what exactly is `softrelu`? Maybe good to add formulas for each of the activation type.
- [x] See also: add link to `LeakyReLU` for people looking for leaky activations.
### BatchNorm
- [ ] Details: provide formula and reference. See e.g. [the torch doc](https://github.com/torch/nn/blob/master/doc/simple.md#nn.BatchNormalization)
- [ ] Shared statistics: do we support BN with shared statistics for RNNs now? If yes, document it.
### BlockGrad
- [ ] Details: give an example use case in RNN unrolling.
### Cast
### Concat
- [ ] `data` and `num_args`: since we specialized the calling convention for this kind of operators. It might be good to also specialize their auto-gen docstring so that they are less confusing.
### Convolution
### Correlation
- [ ] Details: provide formula or more explanation?
### Crop
- [ ] Formats: punctuations and spaces are missing. E.g. "... will be used This function support ...", "... we will use the h_wfor crop height ..."
- [ ] Examples: maybe need examples. I'm a bit confused by "2nd and 3rd dim", is it counting from 0 or 1?
### Custom
- [ ] Details: maybe add link to documentations on how to implement custom operators.
### Deconvolution
- [ ] Details: maybe add formula and references. I often find it is very confusing when different toolkits use different names for similar things like deconvolution, unconvolution, convolution_transpose, dialated convolution, etc.
### Dropout

Ref: #3566 
- [x] Details: I guess everybody knows dropout, but adding a formula is not bad.
### ElementWiseSum
- [ ] Doc: not sure why this operator is not taking a `data` argument. Maybe examples are needed.
- [ ] Clarification: what is the difference between this and `+`? Does this do something special?
### Embedding

Ref #3596
- [x] Details and examples: I find the doc quite confusing until I check the code in RNN examples. The inputs are not one-hot encoded, but just integer indices. The `input_dim` is the input vocabulary size, while "input dim of one-hot encoding" sounds like the axis of the multi-dim tensor that is used to do one-hot encoding.
### Flatten
- [x] Details
### FullyConnected
### IdentityAttachKLSparseReg
- [ ] Details: formula and references (if any)
### L2Normalization
- [ ] Details: "Set the l2 norm of each instance to a constant", but to what constant? 
### LRN
- [ ] Details: the doc sounds like that this is just a convolution operator.
### LeakyReLU
- [ ] Details: formula for each activation type.
### LinearRegressionOutput
- [ ] Details: formula
### LogisticRegressionOutput
- [ ] Details: formula
### MAERegressionOutput
- [ ] Details: formula
### MakeLoss
- [ ] Example
### Pooling
- [ ] Details: document expected input and output shape, add examples
### RNN
- [ ] Details: formula for each cell type?
### ROIPooling
- [ ] Details: maybe link to some references and example directory?
### Reshape
- [ ] Format: `shape` argument missing type
### SVMOutput
- [ ] Details
### SequenceLast
### SequenceMask
### SequenceReverse
### SliceChannel
### Softmax
- [ ] Maybe should remove this at some point? Deprecated for a long time.
### SoftmaxActivation
### SoftmaxOutput
- [ ] Fix: "If set to true, for a (n,k,x_1,..,x_n) dimensional input tensor, softmax will generate n_x_1_...*x_n output", does not seem to be correct, the output is still the same shape as the input, just normalized along one axis.
### SpatialTransformer
- [ ] Details: reference and example?
### SwapAxis
- [ ] Details
### UpSampling
- [ ] Related issue: https://github.com/dmlc/MXNet.jl/issues/108

issue_title: MXNet 0.11.0 Release Feedback: Source Headers and License Files
issue_body:
As discussed in various email threads - [Voting thread](https://lists.apache.org/thread.html/171c0ff73e5f7539611a8024fef42968f5ffea800c354b2cdfa04ab2@%3Cgeneral.incubator.apache.org%3E), [Source Headers](https://lists.apache.org/thread.html/78b01bc01e107300257ca2bc7ad04c7eae8c87bcc4cffaaf829813bd@%3Cgeneral.incubator.apache.org%3E) and [ICLAs vs Contributors](https://lists.apache.org/thread.html/d88544cc13275c502a1580b16b0575f9d827a0041c0bd2879b7a9197@%3Cdev.mxnet.apache.org%3E)

**1. Headers**
a) "The source headers should be reverted in any areas where not all contributors to that file have signed ICLAs, otherwise we need some kind of agreement they are OK with moving to NOTICE file (which is how this has been handled in the past)"
b) While a number of file (around 50 odd) do have "Copyright (c) 2016 by Contributors” they don’t have a full Apache licensed header please fix this in the next release. More files exist with other years and without the correct header.


**2. License Files**
a) Missing - BSD license for this file [4]
b) Missing - MIT licensed JQuery and Sizzle.js [5]
c) Missing - This BSD licensed file [6]
d) Missing - Boost Software licensed file [7]
e) Missing - This BSD licensed files [8][9]
f) This license file may be a little misleading [1] where its apache license but also claims “All rights reserved”. I notice this in a number of other places as well.
g) A number of Apache licenses have incorrect appendixes with the copyright line filled in e.g. [2]
h) This file may incorrect have an ASF header on it [3]


**3. Files referred above can be found as follows** - 

[1] ./src/operator/contrib/ctc_include/LICENSE
[2]../dlpack/LICENSE
[3] ./example/image-classification/predict-cpp/image-classification-predict.cc
[4] ./docs/_static/searchtools_custom.js
[5] ./docs/_static/jquery-1.11.1.js
[6] ./cub/test/mersenne.h
[7] ./cmake/Modules/FindJeMalloc.cmake
[8] ./dmlc-core/cmake/Modules/FindCrypto.cmake
[9] ./nnvm/dmlc-core/cmake/Modules/FindCrypto.cmake

issue_title: jsonref dereference happening two times, leading to failures.
The use of auto_now and auto_now_add
issue_body:
Why was the decision made to switch from `default=datetime_now` to `auto_now=True` and `auto_now_add=True`? I found [this](https://github.com/django-extensions/django-extensions/pull/693) pull request that implements it, but it is not linked to an issue.

I create this issue now because after updating django_extensions we've run into bugs because of this. Also the use of those kwargs is discouraged and the generally accepted sollution to this problem is the way it was before the pull request was merged (using a default value and a pre_save).

Not to mention the migrations everyone has to do after updating (neglectable I know, but nevertheless unnecessary).

issue_title: Naive shim around $.data breaks much code
issue_body:
The shim around .data doesn't support the same set of overloads as the core function. It assumes the second parameter is the `name` property, whereas it can be an object to set a bunch of properties simultaneously.

See: https://github.com/jquery/jquery-migrate/blob/master/src/data.js#L7
vs: https://github.com/jquery/jquery/blob/master/src/data.js

Among other things, this breaks AngularJS.

issue_title: un-gitignore bower_components
issue_body:
@vangheem @pilz @bloodbare @jensens 

I dislike the Products.CMFPlone static/components folder, which is most often not in sync with current mockup development.

What about removing it and instead git-commit-push mockups bower_components (possibly renamed to just ocmponents)? mockup is a core dependency of Plone anyways, and the 50 MB payload shouldn't be that problem. We would not need to keep cmfplone's components directory in sync with mockups. And we might possibly make plone.patternslib obsolete, because we already have these in mockup.

Further thinking, maybe we should then move all resource definitions from CMFPlone's dependencies/registry.xml into a newly to-be-created mockup profile.

I would also move the toolbar pattern into mockup.

Anything against something like this?

I would target Plone 5.1 - so now it's the time for it.

issue_title: import deadlock in pyzmq if used in another thread
issue_body:
If we have the following code
file1.py:
```python
import zmq
from threading import Thread
context = zmq.Context.instance()
socket = context.socket(zmq.PUB)
socket.connect('tcp://localhost:12345')
#socket.send_json('{uncomment_and_work:True}')
class importLock(Thread):
    def run(self):
        socket.send_json('{}')

a = importLock()
a.start()
```
and run it from file2.py

`import file1`
This will cause the deadlock in python. If we uncommon `send_json` before spawning new thread it will not cause deadlock. 

This is because of PR #721  and this [commit](https://github.com/zeromq/pyzmq/pull/721/files#diff-f838118f708bb4dea24b82532d851df1R451) that we import inside the function. 
Importing in function is bad practice and not [PEP8 ](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/#imports), this change can cause the deadlocks to clients and my suggestion is to remove it. 

I don't mind to open a new PR that will revert the change, but before that, I wanted to consult here and hear your opinion on the matter.

Cheers

issue_title: Add methods to test dates/other EDTFs against EDTF objects
issue_body:
At the moment there are basic equal, less-than or greater-than comparison operators that perform simple comparisons. However, each `EDTFObject` can potentially be a fuzzy, discontinuous range of dates. We can't currently use them for much more than bounding-box calculations.

It would be useful to be able to test a date such as `date(1963, 01, 17)` against the EDTFObject such as `'[1962, 1964]` and have it return `False`, since the date is not in the (discontinuous) range specified.

Something like:
```
>>> e = parse_edtf("[1962, 1964]")
>>> f = parse_edtf("1963")
>>> f in e 
False
>>> f not in e
True
>>> g = parse_edtf("1962-08-28/1963-08-27")
>>> g in e # f is not wholly contained in e
False
>>> g.overlaps(e)
1 # overlaps the end
>>> e.overlaps(g)
-1 # overlaps the beginning
```
etc.

issue_title: Remove the two-phase constraint on the event based calculators
issue_body:
Right now the event based calculators have a serious architectural constraint: they are split in two phases, 1) rupture generation and 2) gmf generation. This architecture causes the following issues:

a) we may run out of memory when collecting the whole set of ruptures at the end of phase 1)
b) the calculation may become ultra-slow when saving and then reading the ruptures between phases 1) and 2)
c) rabbitmq may fail while transferring the ruptures at the beginning of phase 2)

The solution is to generate the ruptures and use them within the same task (single phase calculation). This is the way the UCERF risk calculator works. However, the present UCERF risk calculator parallelizes by source model branch: this means that when there is a single branch it will use a single core, i.e. it is practically useless.

Why we have the two-phase architecture? Because we lack a *provider of unique event IDs*. Currently in phase 1) we generate bogus event IDs which are replaced by sequential event IDs only after, when the events are stored. In order to avoid storing the events or keeping them in memory all at once, we need to set the event IDs before, in the workers. An easy solution is to have the task number inside the ID (for instance the first two bytes could be the `task_no`).

Incidentally, by having a provider of event IDs it will become possible to split the UCERF sources in ruptures and to parallelize well even in the case of a single source model branch.

issue_title: _element should become part of the official API (allows Selenium calls)
issue_body:
Let's look at some code similar to something I write using Splinter:

```
a = browser.find_by_id("foo")
txt = a._element.get_attribute('outerHTML')
try:
    txt.find('some HTML')
except:
   print("Test FAIL")
   return -1
```

The point being, I'm using _element to access a method in the underlying Selenium that Splinter does not appear to support.

We should make this part of the official API: If you want to do something with an element and Splinter does not support it, use _element to make the required Selenium call. This way, already written code does not need to be revised.

issue_title: Improve naive/aware DateTime objects and documentation
issue_body:
I think it would help to clarify [in the documentation](https://docs.pylonsproject.org/projects/colander/en/latest/api.html#colander.DateTime) that a deserialized `datetime` object is by default _aware_ (i.e. its `tzinfo` attribute is set to a timezone object provided by the [`pytz`](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/pytz) non-standard library) rather than being _naive_ (i.e. its `tzinfo` attribute is `None`).

In this context, **normalizing** while deserializing an _aware_ `datetime` into a _naive_ datetime would be useful for those applications which operate purely on _naive_ `datetime` objects. For example, deserializing a string with `+00:00` as a timezone might simply strip `tzinfo`, whereas other offsets ought to be added to a _naive_ `datetime` using a [`timedelta`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.timedelta) derived from the offset.

issue_title: "Meet the Repo type" example improvement
issue_body:
New to this library and just want to ask for feedback before submitting a PR.  

The "Meet the Repo type" example below shows how to create an instance of `Repo`, but it does so by depending on an already created `Repo` instance in some bespoke outer scope.  It then spends 3 lines of comments explaining the what `rorepo` is, and that it's completely unnecessary for the example.  Most importantly, the user cannot simply copy and paste the example and run it locally. 

```
from git import Repo

# rorepo is a Repo instance pointing to the git-python repository.
# For all you know, the first argument to Repo is a path to the repository
# you want to work with
repo = Repo(self.rorepo.working_tree_dir)
assert not repo.bare
```

Is there a reason this example is not something simpler like below?
```
import os
from git import Repo

repo = Repo(os.getcwd())
assert not repo.bare
```

issue_title: Cannot sort NestedModelAdmin itself in changelist view
issue_body:
Django-suit was great...
-------------------------------
Using `django-suit` and *SortableModelAdmin*/*SortableTabularInline*, it is possible to sort objects in the admin interface's changlelist view and sort inlines, provided that a **sortable** attribute is provided ('order' by default in `django-suit`).

```python
class CustomServiceProductBindingInline(suit.admin.SortableTabularInline):
    model = Product.bound_services.through
    verbose_name = _("attached services")
    sortable = 'order'

class ProductModelAdmin(suit.admin.SortableModelAdmin):
    model = Product
    sortable = 'order'
    inlines = [CustomServiceProductBindingInline,]
```

But...
-------
But `django-suit` cannot handle nested inlines, so I integrated properly the `django-nested-admin` application in my web app and it works like a charm. I had to switch my ModelAdmins from *suit.admin.SortableModelAdmin* to *nested_Admin.NestedModelAdmin* and switch my inlines from *suit.admin.SortableTabularInline* to *nested_Admin.NestedTabularInline*...

and the nested inlines work fine....

The problem is : with `django-nested-admin`, my **ModelAdmin cannot be sorted in the changelist view anymore !** Is there a way to implement it ? Is this a compatibility issue with `django-suit` ? Or simply is it not implemented in `django-nested-admin` ?

```python
class CustomServiceProductBindingInline(nested_admin.NestedTabularInline):
    model = Product.bound_services.through
    verbose_name = _("attached services")
    sortable_field_name = "order"


class ProductModelAdmin(nested_admin.NestedModelAdmin):
    model = Product
    sortable_field_name = 'order'
    sortable = 'order'  # Does not work anymore
    sortable_field_name = "order"  # Apparently ignored too...
    inlines = [CustomServiceProductBindingInline,]
```

issue_title: new cli syntax
issue_body:
As discussed in https://github.com/mapbox/rio-color/issues/5, We ditched the idea of click command options (`--saturation`, etc) because they are unordered. As an alternative, we developed a little domain specific language where each additional argument was an operation to applied in order.

e.g. this would apply gamma to red and green then a saturation to the whole image
```
rio color in.tif out.tif "gamma r,g 1.05" "saturation 125"
```

This works until you start passing around the the operations as shell variables. You quickly get into **quote escape and delimeter hell**. Consider this 

```bash
$ color_formula="\"gamma r,g 1.05\" \"saturation 125\""
$ set -x
$ echo $color_formula
+ echo '"gamma' r,g '1.05"' '"saturation' '125"'
```
Ugh. broken by spaces. It gets even nastier when you try to read operations from files or sed streams.

In short, our DSL has issues.

❓ Potential Solutions:
* Use a different delimiter: `gamma_r,g_1.05 saturation_125` a bit yicky but functional. By using `_` and avoiding the space, we avoid the need to use quotes. And we could make it compatible with the existing language.
* Use something that would be an entire departure
    - a single quoted series of S-expressions: `"(gamma r,g 1.05) (saturation 125)"`
    - override `click.Command.parse_args` to keep ordered options: `--gamma r,g 1.05 --saturation 125`
    - unquoted DSL: `gamma r,g 1.05 saturation 125`

/cc @sgillies @celoyd

issue_title: [Error] Problem after `pipenv --update` to latest version (Debian 9, Python 3.5.3)
Ability to load PIPENV_* environs from .env file
issue_body:
I understand the existence of #902, but the it seems the OP was looking for something different.

Sometimes I want Pipenv to handle one particular project differently than others. The most common problem is I don’t usually set `PIPENV_VENV_IN_PROJECT`, but for some projects I want to do. This is however not possible, because they are loaded for `shell` and `run`, not `install` etc., and even if they are, `.env` is loaded during runtime, therefore doesn’t affect values in `environments.py`.

`direnv` and similar tools like `autoenv` are not an option for me because they are all shell-depedent, and I operate in the command prompt on Windows.

My current workaround is to use [a custom Dotenv loader](https://github.com/uranusjr/python-dotenv-run), and a Batch script like this:

```batch
REM pipenv.bat
REM Shim to wrap Pipenv invocation in a loaded environment.
REM Put this in the project root so it’s picked up when you call "pipenv".

if exist ".env" (
    dotenv-run pipenv %*
) else (
    pipenv %*
)
```

which sort of works, but is suboptimal. I wish Pipenv could handle this more cleanly since it already loads .env files anyway.

##### Steps to replicate

1. Create a project
2. Run `pipenv install` with `PIPENV_VENV_IN_PROJECT` set
3. Unset `PIPENV_VENV_IN_PROJECT`, create `.env` with `PIPENV_VENV_IN_PROJECT=1`

##### Expected result

```batch
> pipenv install
Loading .env environment variables…
Installing dependencies from Pipfile.lock
```

Pipenv should go on reusing `.venv` and perform operations.

##### Actual result

```batch
> pipenv install
Creating a virtualenv for this project…
```

Pipenv goes on to create a virtualenv in the default location, not respecting values in `.env`.

issue_title: [ENH] Considering dependencies from requirements-dev.txt
issue_body: I know this isn't standardised, but a lot of projects do use a `requirements-dev.txt` file. Would it makes sense to create the `Pipfile` with `dev-packages` extracted from this too?

issue_title: Idempotent pipenv?
issue_body:
Greetings and thank you for this sweet tool, Kenneth! 

I have a question as I try to integrate `pipenv` into our `make` environment... Is there a way to make `pipenv` act w/idempotence?

Ex: With our current setup, we know that `venv/bin/activate` depends on `setup.py`, `requirements.txt`, etc, and we have a well-constructed Makefile such that if those files have not been updated, then re-creating the virtualenv is a no-op (`make: Nothing to be done for 'venv'.`). The virtual environment will only get constructed if `venv/bin/activate` does not exist, or if any of those course files are newer.

I can't seem to figure out how to get `pipenv` to behave this way. ie. Don't do anything if the virtualenv is already in-line with `Pipfile.lock`. `pipenv install` takes a good amount of time to return, and `pipenv update` does a purge before reinstalling everything.


##### Describe your environment

1. MacOS Sierra
1. Python 3.6.3
1. `pipenv, version 8.3.2`

##### Expected result

A super-fast command, where pipenv can check what is in `Pipfile.lock` and compare with the existing virtualenv.

##### Actual result

I don't know of a way to do this.

##### Steps to replicate

Run `pipenv install` repeatedly. If it's a big project each invocation can take some time.

issue_title: pipenv lock --keep-outdated doesn't update dependencies with extra details
issue_body:
Running `pipenv lock --keep-outdated` when a dependency with extras has changed in the Pipfile doesn't update the Pipfile.lock. **Steps to replicate:** Use these initial files:
Pipfile ``` [[source]] url = "https://pypi.python.org/simple" verify_ssl = true name = "pypi" [packages] flask = "==1.0.*" raven = {extras = ["flask"], version = "==5.27.1"} ```

issue_title: Dependencies could not be resolved when installing both ccxt and onetoken packages.
issue_body:
##### Issue description

Dependencies could not be resolved when installing both ccxt and onetoken packages.

* latest ccxt requires 'aiohttp>=3.0.1'. (https://github.com/ccxt/ccxt/blob/master/python/setup.py)
* latest onetoken requires 'aiohttp==3.1.3'. (https://github.com/1token-trade/onetoken-py-sdk/blob/master/setup.py)
* latest aiohttp requires 'async_timeout>=3.0,<4.0'. (https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/blob/master/setup.py)
* aiohttp v3.1.3 requires 'async_timeout>=1.2,<3.0'. (https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/blob/v3.1.3/setup.py)
* 'aiohttp==3.1.3' is specified in Pipfile. 'async-timeout==2.0.1' is installed. This combination matches the requirements and should work.

##### Expected result

`pipenv update` should succeed with no error.

##### Actual result

```

Running $ pipenv lock then $ pipenv sync.

Locking [packages] dependencies...

There are incompatible versions in the resolved dependencies.
```

issue_title: Provide a way to force ignore a mismatch when locking
issue_body:
It would be great to be able to force an ignore of a mismatch in package dependencies. At present you can't lock a configuration which has has a mismatch, even if the code works properly.

##### Describe your environment

1. OS Type: OSX
1. Python version: 3.6.3
1. Pipenv version: 9.0

##### Expected result

I would like to be able to run `pipenv lock --ignore-mismatch` to create a lockfile which ignores a mismatch generated in version dependencies. Some unhelpful packages include a maximum version in their `install_requires`, even though the version they're preventing you from working with doesn't exist yet.

##### Example

[django-babel](https://github.com/python-babel/django-babel/blob/0.6.1/setup.py#L27) specifies a requirement for `Django<1.12`. The code works absolutely fine with Django 2.0, so this restriction is not relevant. At present the following sequence of commands will fail, and there's no way I can find to generate a lock file to solve it.

```
pipenv install django-babel
pipenv install "Django>=2.0"
```

issue_title: Option to strip binaries generated by auditwheel
issue_body:
Auditwheel is a great tool and is used by a lot of common libraries to build their wheel packages.  I'm most interested in numpy and [they mentioned](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/7570#issuecomment-288260162) that they use this library to package their wheels.  Unfortunately somewhere in their build process the binaries are [modified by patchelf such that strip doesn't work](https://github.com/NixOS/patchelf/issues/10).  

Is it possible to add the option to strip binaries directly within the auditwheel process?  

Stripped binaries are critical for deployment, particularly of lambda functions and I'm unable to do the strip myself once installed.

A lot of people would benefit from this change in terms of saved space in deployment.

issue_title: parse() returns naive timezone when tz is available
issue_body:
parse() ignores timezones by default.  It should maintain the timezone.

```python
>>> from dateutil.parser import parse
>>> parse('Wed, 11 May 2016 14:30:00 -0400')
datetime.datetime(2016, 5, 11, 14, 30, tzinfo=tzoffset(None, -14400))
>>> import dateparser
>>> dateparser.parse('Wed, 11 May 2016 14:30:00 -0400')
datetime.datetime(2016, 5, 11, 18, 30)
>>>
```

issue_title: Dumper must not add trailing line break at the end
issue_body: Dumper always adds one trailing line break at the end. It was a feature before we added block scalars support, but now it break dumps in cases, when last object in the stream is `|+` or `>+` scalar.

issue_title: Logger logs to worker log and executor log
issue_body:
Hello,

I'm using streamparse 1.1.0 and all our bolts and spouts classes initialize a logger object. While streamparse creates and logs to streamparse_topologyname_modulename_taskid_pid.log, it seems to be sending the same log output to the (java) worker log. This results in worsening of performance. Sample log message from the worker log:

INFO:SomeName:[1.0.0] some log message

Separately, our classes use external packages which seem to be logging differently. (using their own formatter?). For e.g.:
94968 [Thread-18-SPOUT/BOLT] INFO  NAME_OF_LAST_BOLT_IN_TOPOLOGY_CLJ - WARNING:MODULE_NAME:some log message

So the issues are:

1. (High Priority) How to avoid logging to the worker log?
2. Can the solution to above be achieved without upgrading streamparse?
3. In the second log message, even though the SPOUT/BOLT name is correct, the formatter seems to use the module name of the last bolt specified. Why is that?

Thank you very much.

issue_title: [WIP] Add ofilter?
issue_body:
Don't know if this will ever happen, but if it is supposed to happen, I am opening an issue here so it is not forgotten. issue_title: UpdateableAPIResource.update() drops APIResources in the params silently
issue_body:
I was trying to update a managed account's bank account with a token but it gets stripped out - it does not show in the POST params in the dashboard's log. If I use the token's id, it works. This contrasts with CreateableAPIResource.create() which does not call self.serialize() and instead rely's on the request() call to serialize the params. In that case it ends up in api_requestor._api_encode() where any APIResources entities are replaced with their id string.

In code, this works:

    account.bank_account = token.id
    account.save()

and this does not:

    account.bank_account = token
    account.save()

where token is generated from stripe.Token.create(bank_account=<dictwithdeets>)

The docs don't mention having to use the token's id, so I assume this a bug.

issue_title: denoise_tv_bregman returns image as floats greater than 1 (and possibly the opposite) when passing an image as 32-bit floats
issue_body:
I'm using scikit-image for denoising images that are pulled out of caffe.  I believe in my case, the images sent into denoise_tv_bregman are formatted as 32 bit floats.  I've run into a couple errors thrown by img_as_ubyte  after running the image through the denoise_tv_bregman function.

I thought it was an error in img_as_ubyte at first and found the error raised from dtype.py: "Images of type float must be between -1 and 1."  I ended up doing this:

`print str(np.min(image))`
`print str(np.max(image))` 

from the image causing the fault and found that it was within the correct range until it was run through the denoise_tv_bregman function.  I just ended up putting: 

 `image = np.clip(image, 0, 1) `

after calling it but I worry I am losing detail from precision loss and in this case rounding related to using float32 over float64 and obviously by having to clip the data.  After clipping, it seems like the images are fine but I'm sure visually its probably miniscule and I don't want to introduce noise/irregularities since I'm using this function specifically to remove artifacts caused by processing in caffe.

Thank you!  I hope my description is well explained.

Use-cases:
* answers question "How long do I have to train on this dataset?"
* debugging SP,TP,TM implementations that produce SDRs

How?
* define & collect requirements on SDRs (sparsity, distributed) 
* allows new "extensions" to SDR requirements, eg `Good` and `Bad` have SDRs as far from each other as possible
* compute quality for a single SDR vector - range `[0;1]`
  * for a stream of SDRs - we might want to achieve eg certain max overlap over all SDRs, ...

Implementation: 
* as a static function `computeSDRQuality()` somewhere
* as a property in each of SP,TP,TM..
* as a region that connects SP,TP,TM as its input (seems best option to me)

issue_title: MsgPrint should have at least one field.
Python examples don't respond to a keyboard interrupt (CTRL-C)
issue_body:
For any example that uses the Handler class as an iterator, the code seems to swallow any ctrl-c interrupts.  For example, code like this will not respond to CTRL-C:

```python
# Open a connection to Piksi using TCP
with TCPDriver(args.host, args.port) as driver:
    with Handler(Framer(driver.read, None, verbose=True)) as source:
        try:
            for msg, metadata in source.filter(SBP_MSG_BASELINE_NED):
                # Print out the N, E, D coordinates of the baseline
                print "%.4f,%.4f,%.4f" % (msg.n * 1e-3, msg.e * 1e-3,
                                          msg.d * 1e-3)
        except KeyboardInterrupt:
            pass
```

If the example is changed to something like this, it will respond to CTRL-C:

```python
with TCPDriver(args.host, args.port) as driver:
    with Handler(Framer(driver.read, None, verbose=True)) as source:
        try:
            while True:
                source.wait_callback(message_callback)
        except KeyboardInterrupt:
            pass
```

issue_title: Project announcement space
issue_body:
Problem
----------
Invenio the software is adopted by several services at CERN and around, that are building on top of it additional functionalities (e.g. in the form of additional Invenio components). Some of these functionalities might be of interest to other services, who might wish to chime in the design phase, at the very least to better understand what a given service has in mind and to see for potential synergies or energy saving.

As of today every service has typically a Github organization which is being populated with these additional components, and it's difficult or sometimes happen too late, for a service A to know what component service B plans to work on (or is already implementing).

Proposals
-----------

1. Keep the *status quo*, best-effort based: projects are sometimes presented at Invenio forum in a given phase, they are sometimes discovered through Gitter chat, they are sometimes discovered via Github watch list
2. Use the mailing-list to announce a new project
3. Introduce stub project under Inveniosoftware umbrella where services can open issues to announce their projects, containing at the very minimum a link towards some internal issue or even Google Doc, or repo.
4. more?

issue_title: RFC: moving json column to jsonb
issue_body:
Proposal
======

Move `RecordMetadata` json column to `JSONB` when using `postgresql`.

The following code:

```
json = db.Column(
        JSONType().with_variant(
            postgresql.JSON(none_as_null=True),
            'postgresql',
        ),
        default=lambda: dict(),
        nullable=True
    )
```

Can be converted to

```

json = db.Column(
        db.JSON().with_variant(
            postgresql.JSONB(none_as_null=True),
            'postgresql',
        ),
        default=lambda: dict(),
        nullable=True
    )
```

This will allow to use native `jsonb` column in PostgreSQL (see https://www.postgresql.org/docs/9.4/static/datatype-json.html) with advantages such as better indexing capabilities and using containment operators.

-----------

I have tested locally with `PostgreSQL v9.5` and:

```
Flask-SQLAlchemy==2.2
SQLAlchemy==1.1.10
SQLAlchemy-Continuum==1.3.0
SQLAlchemy-Utils==0.32.14
```

And everything seems to work as expected, enabling queries such as:

```
db.session.query(RecordMetadata).filter(type_coerce(RecordMetadata.json, JSONB()).contains({"control_number": 1498185})).all()
```

(Note that `type_coerce` is needed because of the `with_variant` construct - [more info](https://bitbucket.org/zzzeek/sqlalchemy/issues/3855/comparator-on-variant-field)

Which translates to:

```
SELECT *
FROM records_metadata
WHERE records_metadata.json @> '{"control_number": 1498185}'
```

----------------------------

The only thing missing to be done is to write an Alembic recipe to migrate the column.

cc @lnielsen

issue_title: Increase log level for bad packets
issue_body: Currently they're log_debug only, which makes debugging difficult due to debug logging being disabled by default. Upgrade to info level.

issue_title: Improve support for integer-like types
issue_body:
The type registration system currently only uses `__mro__`, which means it doesn't work with ABCs like `numbers.Integral`. In Python 3, types like `numpy.int64` don't inherit from `int`, so they currently crash if set as sensor values or otherwise used in katcp messages.

One approach is to change the type registration system to work with `isinstance` and to register ABCs instead of concrete classes. That needs some investigation because mypy doesn't work well with the `numbers` ABCs.

An alternative is to modify the sensor class to cast value updates to the sensor's registered type. That would fix the problem of setting a sensor to a numpy integer (but with the side effect that reading back the value gives you a different type of object from what you set), but wouldn't help for other messages.

issue_title: Enhancement: Get domain for email from request
issue_body:
We've got 100's of tenants running out of a single settings.py file.  Having settings.DOMAIN provided in settings.py is pretty limiting.  Suggestion would be to pull the domain from the request.  There may be a better way though.

Here is the change I made to utils.py

def get_email_context(self, user):
        token = self.token_generator.make_token(user)
        uid = encode_uid(user.pk)
        # Pull domain from request
        try:
            domain = self.request.get_host()
        except:
            domain = settings.get('DOMAIN')
        return {
            'user': user,
            'domain': domain, # Use domain from request or if not provided fall back to settings.
            'site_name': settings.get('SITE_NAME'),
            'uid': uid,
            'token': token,
            'protocol': 'https' if self.request.is_secure() else 'http',
        }

issue_title: Support backoff for reconnect.
issue_body:
PyMesos currently reconnect to master every 2 seconds (cf: https://github.com/douban/pymesos/blob/master/pymesos/process.py#L246)

This may lead to master pressure, we need add randomized exponentiation backoff in future.

issue_title: Get user instance from JWT not from DB
issue_body:
Hi.
I read this [issue](https://github.com/GetBlimp/django-rest-framework-jwt/issues/192) and still I have some question. How can I do some updating user query without hitting DB for getting user instance? for example my JWT has user_id already inside token. I want to update email field of user and I can do this like this: `User.objects.filter(id=user_id).update(email="example@test.com"), but DRF by default hitting DB and get user instance when send request and we can get user instance like this: request.user. How I can change this behavior and avoiding hitting DB for getting user instance because we already have user_id and can do some stuff with it. thank's

issue_title: Update PyPI package to make it DRF 3.2 compatible
issue_body: Currently, PyPI package (version 1.6.0) doesn't work with DRF 3.2 due to the removal of the deprecated `request.DATA`. There's [a one week old commit](https://github.com/GetBlimp/django-rest-framework-jwt/commit/6d8614268d60f894dad026b5603c8a2fefc35e2f) available so it's only about pushing the update to PyPI.

issue_title: Encryption of user password in request
issue_body:
Maybe I've missed something, but shouldn't there be a possibility to encrypt or hash the password on the client side before sending it to the REST endpoint? When you don't have a TLS encrypted connection this could add some security.
Of course you would then have to compare the encrypted password directly with the one from the django database.

issue_title: Letting you know about the existence of Qt.py
issue_body:
Hi qtpy!

Me and fellow developers are working on a shim much like yours, except with a different mindset and target audience (i.e. film and visual effects). We aren't yet sure whether our approach is better or worse than yours, but expect changes to eventually occur relative our narrow target audience to conflict with those of yours (assuming yours is the Spider IDE and/or the general public, though do correct me if I'm wrong!).

- https://github.com/mottosso/Qt.py

Our goals are to:

1. Remain a [single-file](https://github.com/mottosso/Qt.py/blob/master/Qt.py) implementation.
1. Enable code written explicitly for PySide2 to run on any other binding.
2. Not implement anything; just [remap](https://github.com/mottosso/Qt.py/blob/master/Qt.py#L66) Qt 4 bindings to Qt 5 (again, to PySide2 specifically)
3. [Document](https://github.com/mottosso/Qt.py/blob/master/README.md) and aggressively test [differences](https://github.com/mottosso/Qt.py/blob/master/CAVEATS.md).

We'll be keeping an eye on your developments and hope our coexistence will make us both stronger. I hope we can be friends!

issue_title: quantile_calc with equal weight factor
issue_body:
The `quantile_calc` function in performance.py crashes when you provide it with exact equally weighted factors, as the bin edges are not considered unique. 

    def quantile_calc(x, quantiles):
        return pd.qcut(x, quantiles, labels=False) + 1

should become 

    def quantile_calc(x, quantiles):
        return pd.qcut(x.rank(method='first'), quantiles, labels=False) + 1

since the ranking function will make the qcut understand some type of uniqueness between the bins, and therefore just make the cut where it wants to.

issue_title: Add force_stepk/force_maxk/ii_pad_factor kwargs to PhaseScreenPSF and OpticalPSF.
issue_body:
`PhaseScreenPSF` currently accepts `_force_stepk` and `_force_maxk` kwargs to forward to `InterpolatedImage`.  `OpticalPSF` is really just a wrapper around `PhaseScreenPSF`, so it should get these kwargs too.

Both `PhaseScreenPSF` and `OpticalPSF` also accept a `pad_factor` kwarg which governs the transformation from pupil space to real space.  It would also be nice to be able to regulate the real space -> Fourier space transformation by forwarding a `pad_factor` kwarg to `InterpolatedImage`.  I *think* these two `pad_factor`s should be distinct, so I'm inclined to call the kwargs that gets forwarded from `PhaseScreenPSF` and `OpticalPSF` to `InterpolatedImage` `ii_pad_factor` or something like that.

issue_title: test_wfirst.py currently fails
issue_body:
Running test_wfirst.py from the command line to get the extra, slightly more time-consuming tests is currently failing:
```
$ python test_wfirst.py
time for test_wfirst_wcs = 1.04
time for test_wfirst_backgrounds = 0.08
time for test_wfirst_bandpass = 0.06
time for test_wfirst_detectors = 0.02
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "test_wfirst.py", line 519, in <module>
    test_wfirst_psfs()
  File "test_wfirst.py", line 468, in test_wfirst_psfs
    filename=test_file)
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/galsim/wfirst/wfirst_psfs.py", line 319, in storePSFImages
    method='no_pixel')
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/galsim/chromatic.py", line 2003, in drawImage
    wmult, integrator, self.gsparams)
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/galsim/utilities.py", line 932, in __call__
    result = self.user_function(*key)
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/galsim/chromatic.py", line 1805, in _get_effective_prof
    method='no_pixel')
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/galsim/chromatic.py", line 1002, in drawImage
    int_im = self._get_interp_image(bandpass, image=image, integrator=integrator, **kwargs)
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/galsim/chromatic.py", line 930, in _get_interp_image
    np.max(self.waves)))
RuntimeError: Requested wavelength 300.0 is outside the allowed range: 900.0 to 1230.0 nm
```
It looks like this is related to the changes in the default thinning/truncation stuff.  The 900-1230 range was designed to match the wavelength range for Y106, but now that filter goes from 300-2200, although most of those values are zeros.

The wfirst `getBandpasses` now has a kwarg `default_thin_trunc` which defaults to False, but given what this bandpass looks like, it seems like either that should probably default to True or we should store versions of the bandpasses that remove the extraneous zeros.

However, even if I do switch that to True, then it's still not quite right.  I need to change the 900 -> 885 on line [433 of test_wfirst.py](https://github.com/GalSim-developers/GalSim/blob/master/tests/test_wfirst.py#L433).  

And then the test still doesn't pass, but I'm not sure if this is a real problem or not:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "test_wfirst.py", line 519, in <module>
    test_wfirst_psfs()
  File "test_wfirst.py", line 453, in test_wfirst_psfs
    err_msg='PSF at a given wavelength and interpolated chromatic one evaluated at that '
  File "/Users/Mike/lib/python2.7/site-packages/numpy/testing/utils.py", line 892, in assert_array_almost_equal
    precision=decimal)
  File "/Users/Mike/lib/python2.7/site-packages/numpy/testing/utils.py", line 713, in assert_array_compare
    raise AssertionError(msg)
AssertionError: 
Arrays are not almost equal to 3 decimals
PSF at a given wavelength and interpolated chromatic one evaluated at that wavelength disagree.
(mismatch 0.00629881582263%)
 x: array([[  1.295e-06,   3.367e-06,   3.891e-06, ...,   4.184e-06,
          3.012e-06,   1.152e-06],
       [  2.681e-06,   1.978e-06,   1.822e-06, ...,   1.622e-06,...
 y: array([[  7.226e-07,   1.001e-06,   2.299e-06, ...,   2.268e-06,
          8.914e-07,   7.136e-07],
       [  6.530e-07,   8.959e-07,   2.281e-06, ...,   2.115e-06,...
```
It's only 0.006% of the pixels, so my guess is this is just a numerical fluctuation, not an error.

issue_title: demo13.py (WFIRST) does not work with master
issue_body:
I'm working on translating the [`demo13.py`](https://github.com/GalSim-developers/GalSim/blob/master/examples/demo13.py) file to a Jupyter notebook and finding that certain pieces of the API have changed.

First one, on [L252](https://github.com/GalSim-developers/GalSim/blob/master/examples/demo13.py#L252):

```
        # Generate a point source.
        point = galsim.DeltaFunction(flux=1.)
```

Looks like that should be `galsim.Delta()`.

Once I change that, I get as far as [L256](https://github.com/GalSim-developers/GalSim/blob/master/examples/demo13.py#L256):

```
star = galsim.Convolve(point*star_sed, PSF)
```

Which gives me this error:

```

     21     star_sed = galsim.SED(lambda x:1, 'nm', 'flambda').withFlux(1.,filter_)  # Give it unit flux in this filter.
---> 22     star = galsim.Convolve(point*star_sed, PSF)
     23     img_psf = galsim.ImageF(64,64)

-> 1756         if all([obj.separable for obj in self.objlist]):

-> 1756         if all([obj.separable for obj in self.objlist]):

issue_title: Support managing workflow roles
issue_body: This should be straightforward to implement. We don't have it for the simple reason that roles were introduced in 3.0 and workflows were introduced in 3.1, and roles were not updated.

issue_title: Implement infrastruture discovery agent core
issue_body:
Right now we have multiple infrastructure discovery agents used to sync different third-party system components to ZMON entities (e.g. AWS, Kubernetes, GCP ...). Each agent is standalone, however a lot of code is identical/repeated.

The idea is to implement a core agent which can dynamically load plugin(s) to discover different external system components, and the core syncs them with ZMON backend. That way we enable easier integration points with external systems.

Useful here as well: https://github.com/zalando-zmon/zmon-demo/issues/10

issue_title: Support multi sync standbys
issue_body:
Hello,

Now PostgreSQL 9.6 supports having N sync standbys with the following syntax:

`synchronous_standby_names='2(standby1, standby2)'`

Are there any plans to extend Patroni to support this?

Ideally it would be great to have 3 nodes at siteA and 3 nodes at siteB and enforce a sync standby in each site at all times. I would imagine this wouldn't be too large a change?

Cheers,

issue_title: Consider bravado-core to manage OpenAPI/Swagger 2.0 validation/parser
issue_body:
I have found that we are dealing a lot with OpenAPI/Swagger 2.0 Specification validation and parsing in Connexion code. I was willing to implement an library independent of Connexion code that would manage all those reference checks and validations in a way that could be used by Connexion and other packages that want to deal with OpenAPI Specification. I have found that there is already a package that does exactly what I was looking for. It is called [bravado-core](https://github.com/Yelp/bravado-core) by Yelp. 

The question is, have you already considered that before? If so, why not reuse that code? If not, what do you think about that idea?

There are many advantages and disadvantages of going that way. Like the core functionality of Connexion being dependent of an external library being the biggest concern. But there are also great benefits like a lot of work is done ahead on supporting the OpenAPI specification.

issue_title: feature request: DatasetManager.delete
issue_body:
Deleting datasets is an important task, and this library should support it. The call is a 'DELETE' request to `api/v1/viz`.

Like Builder, a call to `DatasetManager.delete` should error/warn if a dataset is connected to a map. I prefer an Error in the Python case. Adding a `force=False|True` will let users force the operation regardless. As far as I know, there's metadata in the api/v1/viz GET that lists the dependents of a table.

issue_title: Compatibility for marshmallow >=3.0.0b7
issue_body:

Marshmallow changed the interface for the `Schema.load()` and `Schema.dump()` methods so argschema_parser's `__init__` is broken.  From mm documentation: "Changed in version 3.0.0b7: This method returns the deserialized data rather than a (data, errors) duple. A ValidationError is raised if invalid data are passed."

    if len(result.errors) > 0:

issue_title: Needless calling of GPU memory allocation
issue_body:
There are a couple issues in the code here:

https://github.com/clab/dynet/blame/master/dynet/model.cc#L809

First, the `sizeof()` call will be returning the size of a pointer, which will almost always be under the required amount of scratch memory. This means that almost every time this will be re-allocating scratch memory for every parameter update.

Second, this probably shouldn't be calling "malloc" at all, but just use the device scratch memory.

issue_title: Vanilla LSTM without a forget bias
issue_body: f727c84e7b0239255a9e5a64a6bec3c3262c5976 added a forget bias to the vanilla LSTM, with a TODO to make it a hyperparameter. I think this TODO should be implemented, as it is not clear that using a forget bias is beneficial, e.g. [Dozat & Manning 2017](https://web.stanford.edu/~tdozat/files/TDozat-CoNLL2017-Presentation.pdf).

issue_title: affine_transform[b, W, x] VS b + W* x
issue_body:
The docs say affine_transform(xs) Return an expression equal to: xs[0] + xs[1]*xs[2] + xs[3]*xs[4] + ...
But it's not absolutely right. The dims of two outputs are not equal.
For example, the following code
```
import dynet as dy
m = dy.Model()
_W = m.add_parameters((4,3))
_b = m.add_parameters(4)
x = dy.inputVector([1,2,3])
W = dy.parameter(_W)
b = dy.parameter(_b)
y = b + W * x
z = dy.affine_transform([b, W , x])
print y.dim(), z.dim()
```
will print  `((4,), 1) ((4, 1), 1)`

issue_title: 'Bucket'/'Blob' do not support 'user_project' during 'update'
issue_body:
Whether due to oversight, or because the `buckets.update` / `objects.update` APIs weren't allowing it during implementation, the `update` methods (implemented in `_helpers._PropertyMixin`) don't pass their object's `user_project` (if set).

See #3474.

See also the `buckets.update` and `objects.update` method descriptions in the [discovery doc](https://www.googleapis.com/discovery/v1/apis/storage/v1/rest), which clearly show that `userProject` should be allowed.

issue_title: PyPI project metadata doesn't specify requires_dist
issue_body:
[poetry](https://github.com/sdispater/poetry)'s dependency resolver uses [PyPI's JSON API](https://wiki.python.org/moin/PyPIJSON), particularly the `info.requires_dist` field, to determine a package's dependencies. [In dill's case](https://pypi.org/pypi/dill/json), this field is null. Because of this, poetry won't pick up the `pyreadlines` dependency when installing dill on Windows.

(see https://github.com/sdispater/poetry/issues/20#issuecomment-374237508 for a bit of similar context)

issue_title: dir_archive implementation issues
issue_body:
If, for example, dir_archive is used with flattened keys the str() encoding of those tuples yields directory names with parenthesis, and these ultimately do not correctly load if the archive is written non-pickled (i.e., as python objects) because invalid characters are used in the import statement that is eval()ed. There also seem to be some hacks relative to reloading the archive from disk, in particular

1) _getkey contains [2:] vs being based on the value of PREFIX
2) the interactions between _getdir _getkey and _lookup make various assumptions which I believe frustrate causing _fname to meaningfully modify the text representation of keys as "good" filenames
3) _lookup in particular does not distinguish between calls made to it where the key parameter is coming from a directory name vs really being a key (sequence is _keydict()-->_getkey()-->_lookup()-->_getdir() ), implying the assumed equivalence of dir and key encodings.

I've fixed some problems in a branch, e.g., by adding a parameter to _lookup(...,isdir=False) allowing me to implement a filename encoding in _fname which eliminates problematic characters. This yields the ability to have relatively clear-text directory names, and python object storage that works. I.e., a disk cache that is easily understood by human eyes. This makes the cache useful as a backing store for, for example, function values used to replay behavior in testing frameworks. E.g., run the program once with nothing in the dir_archive, then run it again from a full dir_archive to regression test the parts that rely on the functions that got cached.

I can submit a pull request, but see 0 pull requests here, so I'm wondering if you're accepting community input here.

... I'm also wondering why _hasinput() doesn't use os.path.isfile().

issue_title: Proper implementation of `**` globs in the v2 engine.
issue_body:
AFAICT, we don't have a proper implementation of trailing `**` matching in the new engine. This needs to be repaired.

Expectation
  `**` -> matches everything, recursively.
  `dir/**` -> matches everything under `dir`, recursively.
  `**/*.py` -> matches every dir recursively, searching for files with a `.py` extension.

It may also make sense to define a formal spec for our globs implementation so we can code to the spec.

References:

- https://github.com/bazelbuild/bazel/blob/master/src/test/java/com/google/devtools/build/lib/vfs/RecursiveGlobTest.java#L58-L76
- https://git-scm.com/docs/gitignore

issue_title: Eliminate `python_test_suite` usage in the pants codebase.
issue_body: These just lead folks astray from using the `target` target alias bag.

issue_title: Refactor export task into multiple tasks
issue_body:
Right now the export task has this weird inheritance: `class ExportTask(IvyTaskMixin, PythonTask)`

This is because it currently emits data about python and jvm builds. But this is fragile: if we add some third language (say, Go) we'll need yet another base class, with all the possible conflicts that might introduce. 

In fact, because this mixes in IvyTaskMixin but cannot subclass NailgunTask, it has to create its own SubprocessExecutor, which is not the natural way of doing things. And this prevents us from simplifying IvyTaskMixin (because we can't assume that it's always mixed into a NailgunTask). 

Instead, we should have export.jvm, export.python etc. that produce products, and then an export task that requires those products and actually emits them in the export format.

issue_title: Contributors cannot add the `reviewable` label

But the `reviewable` label also might be a bit of unnecessary boilerplate, given that the travis checks are a pretty good proxy for which code is ready to review.

If the coveralls check were fixed, the following query would work to identify green PRs:

is:pr is:open status:success
```
... but currently it doesn't match anything, because coveralls fails. Thus, this one is related-to/blocked-on #3836. cc @jsirois

issue_title: deprecate `changed` goal and friends.
issue_body: once the v2 engine is mainlined, we should deprecate the `changed`, `test-changed` and `compile-changed` goals in favor of the `Changed`/`TargetRoots` mechanics (e.g. `./pants --changed-parent=HEAD list` replaces `./pants --changed-parent=HEAD changed`).

issue_title: Switch import of pathlib
issue_body: To make sure we use `pathlib` from the standard library for Python 3.5, the import order in `clldutils.path` should be switched to first try the standard lib module and fall back to `pathlib2` on `ImportError`.

issue_title: Creates default "Stage" and stage named "Stage"?
issue_body:
I have a template like this:

```
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Transform: 'AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31'
Resources:
  Api:
    Properties:
      StageName: dv01
      DefinitionUri: api_spec_SAM.json
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Api
```

After package and deploy with the cli, it creates an API with two stages named "Stage" and "dv01". In reality, I do not want to create either stages. The StageName property is "required". Not sure why. My intention is to create a separate set of templates to create the stages, deployments, lambda function versions etc. 

The reason I need separation is because we have multiple code pipelines that are owned by different people (dev, build teams). I would like these templates to live in separate git repos.

Is there a way to create an API without any stages? 

Else, the work around is to simply ignore this one default stage created via SAM. Thanks!

issue_title: API endpoint to delete content DB from file system
issue_body:
In the current implementation on `import-export`, we use the `ChannelResource` to 'delete' a Channel from the server:

```
// manageContentActions.js line 48
ChannelResource.getModel(channelId).delete()
```

This deletes the Channel's metadata, but not the files comprising the Channel. Apparently, this means if the server is restarted, the Channel metadata will be reconstructed and reappear in the app.

Assuming that 'to delete' means to disable the the channel _and_ remove its contents from the device, we need an API to do the later.

An alternative interpretation of 'to delete' is to simply disable the channel but _not_ remove its contents from the device. This would probably also need another API.

issue_title: The metadata tactic needs de-duplication
issue_body:
I built a charm from several layers and I see all the maintainers are in the resulting `metadata.yaml` file. Some of this is our fault, we spelled our names or emails differently, but the names that are exactly the same should be de-duplicated.

```yaml
"name": "kubernetes"
"summary": "Kubernetes is an application container orchestration platform."
"description": |
  Kubernetes is an open-source platform for deplying, scaling, and operations
  of appliation containers across a cluster of hosts. Kubernetes is portable
  in that it works with public, private, and hybrid clouds. Extensible through
  a pluggable infrastructure. Self healing in that it will automatically
  restart and place containers on healthy nodes if a node ever goes away.
"tags":
- "containers"
- "layer"
- "containers"
- "networking"
- "infrastructure"
- "security"
- "infrastructure"
"requires":
  "etcd":
    "interface": "etcd"
"provides":
  "dockerhost":
    "interface": "dockerhost"
    "scope": "container"
"peers":
  "certificates":
    "interface": "tls"
"maintainers":
- "Charles Butler <charles.butler@canonical.com>"
- "Matt Bruzek <matthew.bruzek@canonical.com>"
- "Charles Butler <charles.butler@canonical.com>"
- "Matthew Bruzek <matthew.bruzek@ubuntu.com>"
- "Matthew Bruzek <matthew.bruzek@canonical.com>"
- "Charles Butler <charles.butler@canonical.com>"
"subordinate": !!bool "false"
```
The **maintainer** and the **tags** values could be de-duplicated. This could be done by casting them to a set or using a loop to detect if a value has already been written by another method.

build: Processing layer: layer:basic

issue_title: Invalid code generation if charm name contains a hyphen
issue_body:
If a layer-based charm name contains a hyphen, the code generated via `charm build` will fail upon deploy.

```python
@when_not('terms-example.installed')
def install_terms-example():
    # Do your setup here.
    set_state('terms-example.installed')
```

The hyphen in the method name should be replaced with an underscore.

issue_title: Stop copying system tests to bin/
issue_body:
We copy system tests at build time, from:

    src/niswitch/system_tests/test_system_niswitch.py

to:

    bin/niswitch/system_tests/test_system_niswitch.py

If memory serves me right, we do this for historical reasons: we used to play import path tricks rather than install the modules so we needed the tests to be in a specific location relative to the built modules. This is no longer true - now we install the modules in the virtual environment. So we should be able to stop copying the files and run them from their location in src/.

issue_title: abort() is not a public method
Verify 32-bit Python 2 is handling ViInt64 correctly
issue_body:
On Python 2, int's size is equals to machine's word size. There's a different type long which can be of any size.
On Python 3, there's no long and int can be of any size.

Our APIs have ViInt64 which is 64-bits. We need to make sure that we are handling values larger than 32-bit correctly in Python 2 32-bit (where word size is 32-bit).

I'm not certain what the best way of doing this is. Possibly we need to inspect Python version and machine bitness and then have class ViInt64 inherit from long rather than int in the Python2 32-bit case.
This would be build/templates/python_types.py in class ViInt64.

issue_title: ApplicationRunner API improvements
issue_body:
Currently, the "usual" way to run an ``ApplicationSession``-derived component is via the ``ApplicationRunner`` API. This lumps together several things:

- creates the component (ComponentConfig + instantiating the session)
- possibly sets up TLS options, if ``wss://`` URI
- starts the event-loop (``reactor.run()`` for Twisted or ``run_until_complete`` in asyncio).
- starts logging (via ``txaio.start_logging``).
- handles several error-cases

This becomes awkward for certain use-cases, and e.g. spawned a ``start_reactor=bool`` kwarg for the Twisted version (which also lumps in logging, because ``start_logging`` is only called if it starts the reactor itself).

- users wanting to start their own logging (e.g. https://github.com/crossbario/txaio/issues/75#issuecomment-216651341)
- users wanting to run the event-loop elsewhere (e.g. ``start_reactor=`` kwarg)
- users who want to handle errors themselves

I am filing this bug to collect these use-cases as a way to improve the "I wish to run a component" API to ensure they're covered.

issue_title: Constraints only considered when there's an equal to symbol included
issue_body:
Consider "x" an integer ranging between 0 to 10.
Objective is: x + 10
Problem is: Minimize
Constraint: x > 4.1
Expected Result: x = 5, obj = 15
Obtained Result: x = 0, obj = 10
Code:
`from pulp import *`
`prob = LpProblem("test1", LpMinimize)`
`x = LpVariable("x", 0, 10, 'Integer')`
`prob += x + 10, "obj"`
`prob += x > 4.1, "c1"`
`prob.solve()`
`print("Status:", LpStatus[prob.status])`
`print(prob.variables()[0].name, "=", prob.variables()[0].varValue)`
`print("objective=", value(prob.objective))`

I've observed that by changing the constraint from ">" to ">=" and it worked as expected.

Code:
`from pulp import *`
`prob = LpProblem("test1", LpMinimize)`
`x = LpVariable("x", 0, 10, 'Integer')`
`prob += x + 10, "obj"`
`prob += x >= 4.1, "c1"   #Changed the constraint from > to >=`
`prob.solve()`
`print("Status:", LpStatus[prob.status])`
`print(prob.variables()[0].name, "=", prob.variables()[0].varValue)`
`print("objective=", value(prob.objective))`
Obtained Result: x = 5, obj = 15

issue_title: Pushing large app crashes host / platform
issue_body:
Deis v1.4.

I got a hung process; deis-store-metadata. At first only a single metadata node went down; but that made deis-logger crash, which meant I couldn't push deploys anymore to the builder (or maybe the builder requires ceph for something; I don't know) -- this was the effect non the less.

```
$ fleetctl list-units
deis-store-gateway@1.service		1084039b.../10.7	inactive	dead
deis-store-metadata.service		1084039b.../10.7	failed		failed
deis-store-metadata.service		180588fb.../10.6	inactive	dead
deis-store-metadata.service		6a224380.../10.105	inactive	dead
```

How to bring it back up?

Logs from `10.6`:

```
$ journalctl -fu deis-store-metadata.service
Apr 25 02:52:52 deis-2 sh[1149]: 2015-04-25 02:52:52.456929 7ffb77f35700  1 mds.-1.0 handle_mds_map standby
Apr 25 08:30:18 deis-2 sh[1149]: 2015-04-25 08:30:18.926682 7ffb77f35700  1 mds.-1.0 handle_mds_map standby
Apr 25 09:08:08 deis-2 systemd[1]: Stopping deis-store-metadata...
Apr 25 09:08:08 deis-2 sh[1149]: 2015-04-25 09:08:08.262425 7ffb7362b700 -1 mds.-1.0 *** got signal Terminated ***
Apr 25 09:08:08 deis-2 sh[1149]: 2015-04-25 09:08:08.262525 7ffb7362b700  1 mds.-1.0 suicide.  wanted down:dne, now up:standby
Apr 25 09:08:08 deis-2 systemd[1]: Stopped deis-store-metadata.

I find it very bad manners for my software to suicide in production, and I think that should be considered a bug!

I also have a terminal program hung on `deisctl restart deis-store-metadata` which gives no intelligible output.

Have you considered implementing something like Zipkin? Because it's really hard to correlate and debug with command line tools inside a cluster. Just an off-the-shelf ELK stack or Greylog2 equivalent would also do just fine.

issue_title: Proposal ref(publisher): change the publication loop logic
issue_body:
Right now the publisher uses a ttl to expire the key of the application if the container and the open are available. This works just fine until you start hundreds of container because at that point the 10 seconds loops never ends.

Why not change the logic to publish the port when the container starts and use the loop to check if the container is and the port opened and remove it if not available?
```
if s.IsPublishableApp(containerName) && !s.IsPortOpen(hostAndPort) {
  s.removeEtcd(keyPath, false)
}
```

With this change the interaction with etcd is just 1 request per application when is not available instead of  `O(n)` every 10 seconds (to update the ttl) (n is the number of apps running in the local node)

issue_title: Oddly, example-ruby-sinatra runs slow with k8s as scheduler
issue_body:
I'm having a tough time getting to the bottom of this.  Opening the issue just to ensure it's tracked.

I've discovered, by chance, that when I deploy the example-ruby-sinatra app using k8s as the scheduler, responses from the application are very slow.  Oddly this does not happen with Fleet as the scheduler _and_ it does not happen with other apps.

Digging in farther, if you `nse` to a container running the sinatra app and `curl -L http://localhost:5000`, it responds very quickly.  If however, you make the request using the IP on the overlay network that Docker has assigned to the container _or_ if you use the IP that k8s uses for the service, then the response takes 10+ seconds.

Compare this to when Fleet is used as the scheduler and requests made using the IP on the overlay network that Docker has assigned to the container are very fast.

Something is fishy and I am still on the hunt.

issue_body:
In some commands the text output is an ascii-art table.  (See #168 about changing this to --format table.)  For example:

$ globus bookmark list
Name                                | Endpoint ID                          | Bookmark ID                          | Path                               
----------------------------------- | ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------ | -----------------------------------
midway/~/myproject                  | af7bda53-6d04-11e5-ba46-22000b92c6ec | d89d697e-8cce-11e5-9971-22000b96db58 | /~/myproject/                      

In some commands the text output is a set of Name:Value pairs, each on their own line.  For example:

$ globus bookmark show 13fddf3e-4e77-11e6-8235-22000b97daec
ID:          13fddf3e-4e77-11e6-8235-22000b97daec
Name:        My favorite project
Endpoint ID: af7bda53-6d04-11e5-ba46-22000b92c6ec
Path:        /~/myproject/

I assume the rule is that multi-record output uses the table format, where-as single-record output uses the Name:Value output.  I think we should be more consistent about this.

I suggest we name each of these output format distinctly: "--format table" vs "--format record".  For the record based output, if there are multiple output records, have a blank link between each record.

Note that record-based output should be consistent with aligning the value or not.  "globus bookmark show" aligns the values so that they all start at the same column.  But "globus whoami --verbose" does not, as it just has a single space between the : and the value.

issue_title: get-identities: remove --usernames & --identities
issue_body:
Why does "globus get-identities" need the --usernames and --identities options?  They have unambiguous syntax.  A username is always user@domain, and an identity id is always a UUID.  The CLI should just figure that out, and not make me add the option.

Note that "globus bookmark show" does essentially the same thing, allowing me to input either a bookmark name or id, but it does not require me to include an option to say which I am inputting.

issue_title: Add `by_scopes` form (or similar) to token response
issue_body:
This is pretty much just another presentation of the same data that we put into `OAuthTokenResponse.by_resource_server`.

Although instinctively I'd just generate this as a dict, I don't think that's right. IMO, `tokens.by_scopes['openid profile']` should do the same thing as `tokens.by_scopes['openid']`
So, `by_scopes` should really be a small class which has `__getitem__` defined as doing `.split(' ')`, getting all indexed values, `KeyError` if num values != 1


We _could_ do this with `tokens.get_by_scopes('openid profile')`, but I don't think this is in any way fundamentally easier than a dict-like with sophisticated access.
Is this too fancy?

issue_title: Predicting Future Values
issue_body:
I am using TSFresh for feature extraction for one of the time series problem.
I have to predict the daily closing balance for around 100 commercial accounts.

Now I train and fit my data till say 12/22 (available data) and have to predict 7 days in future. How do I do this efficiently? 

My Approach: 
a. Predict the target variable (amount) for 12/22. 
b. Append it to my original timeseries and feed it as input to feature extraction process to get relevant features.
c. Train and fit the new model
d. Predict for 12/23
e. Repeat a through d for 7 days


Not sure if this is the best way to approach this situation so reaching out to you to check if there is any function/method with TSFresh that would allow me to save all this computation and fitting over and over again.


Thanks,
Aakash

issue_title: My Beef with EWASM
issue_body:
### Why is this here?

These thoughts are not accessible in a public place, nor have they been well aggregated.

### Why does Piper have concerns about EWASM?

#### Computation Introspection

The EVM design in Py-EVM was created with some explicit design goals.

- Deep introspection of the computation.
- Easy to read EVM implementation for reference purposes.
- Easy to modify EVM for research purposes.

EWASM seems to take all of these off of the table. All of the proposed ways for Py-EVM to use EWASM make computation a black box both in terms of understanding what happened during a computation, as well as reading and understanding the actual implementation.  They also seem to make modifying the EVM significantly more difficult, or even impossible without changes to some external dependency.

#### 3rd party tooling support

There is an interactive debugger for Vyper being built using Py-EVM.  I do not believe this would be possible under the implementation plans I've seen for EWASM.

#### Poor cross language support

There was a grant to create a python web assembly interpreter: https://github.com/poemm/pywebassembly

The resulting code isn't quite something we can use in production.  Alternatives are to delegate to an external implementation using the C implementation.  It seems that other languages would end up having to implement their own interpreters.

This leaves us with the following:  Each EVM implementation must either implement a compliant EWASM interpreter in their language ecosystem **or** submit to the will of an external implementation using something akin to CFFI in python at which point they are no longer in control of their EVM implementation.

And if we all end up using the same implementation, then we're basically accepting that the EVM has a reference implementation which is something I don't think we want.

issue_title: Remove support for `excluded_packages`
issue_body:
The idea behind excluding packages was to allow users to install wagons in environments where some requirements were already installed (whether of the required dependency version or otherwise). Using constraint files, this feature isn't needed anymore and you can simply put a constraint on the already installed package in your environment.

Thus, the feature of excluding packages should be removed.

issue_title: Add support for Django 1.9
Update HStoreWhereNode to follow django's WhereNode
issue_body:
The Constraint class has been deprecated since django17 and was removed in django19 (django/django@5008a4) in favour of the Lookup class, which was introduced in django17 (django/django@20bab2).

Consequently django's WhereNode class looks very different now than it used to and HStoreWhereNode needs to be rewritten to follow suit.

Required action:
-Update HStoreWhereNode to behave like WhereNode in django19, ensuring backward compatibility down to django17.
-Drop django16 support.

issue_title: Improved documentation on the version comparison code
issue_body:
The current conda docs imply that the version comparison code mimics PEP 386:

http://conda.pydata.org/docs/spec.html#build-version-spec

This is out of date. In fact, thanks to @ukoethe, it mimics PEP 440, with some deliberate exceptions.

The [docstring for the VersionOrder class](https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/master/conda/version.py#L21-L127) is probably the best documentation we have for the current implementation, and should be added to the documentation somehow, preferably in its own section.

cc: @jseabold @cel4 @ukoethe #1107 #2297 #2300 #1601

issue_title: Package installs failing on Windows with conda 4.3.x due to cryptography error
issue_body:
Hi all,
I am unable to install my packages from my Anaconda Cloud channel with conda in Windows with conda 4.3.x.

The command `conda create -n bstore -c kmdouglass bstore` results in the following error repeated to the screen a few hundred times:

`extern "Python": function Cryptography_locking_cb() called, but @ffi.def_extern() was not called in the current subinterpreter. Returning 0.`

The final result is that the environment is created but no packages are installed into it. Attempts at manually installing packages one-by-one result in errors stating that the package is not available for win-64, even though they are available. Using conda 4.2.16 instead, everything goes smoothly.

The same conda command in Linux x64 and conda 4.3.8 works perfectly.

I think the issue may be related to this one, but I am not sure: https://github.com/pyca/cryptography/issues/3282

issue_title: Add hash characters to tarball file name and extracted package directory name
issue_body:
Append the first 5 characters of the tarball md5 hash to the tarball file name when it's written to the package cache.  Also use the same file name (i.e. with the appended md5 characters) as the extracted package directory name.

To clarify, for the tarball file name, the hash should be appended to file name root, before the `.tar.bz2` extension.  So if the hash characters `a1b2c` for the file `cryptography-1.5.3-py27_0.tar.bz2`, the filename written to the cache should be `cryptography-1.5.3-py27_0-a1b2c.tar.bz2` and the extracted directory name should be `cryptography-1.5.3-py27_0-a1b2c`.

I think the `-` separator is correct here.  If it causes a problem with people string parsing file names for information they should be getting from metadata anyway, ¯\\\_(ツ)\_/¯.

issue_title: Only triple-quoted values get unquoted.
issue_body:
Quotes are ending up as part of the value string in most cases. It's not clear what the intended behavior is, but there's at least some inconsistency. A value like this…
```
clean_command       = "hi"
```
…doesn't have its quotes stripped. It ends up as `'clean_command': '"hi"'` in the final dict. However, a triple-quoted string (either single or double) like this…
```
clean_command       = """hi"""
```
…works just fine: `'clean_command': 'hi'`.

My preference would be to have the quotes stripped off in both cases.

Thanks for maintaining configobj!

issue_title: Add sv rank model version to case page
Case report fails for some cases
SWIG wheel building failed due to syntax error
issue_body:
I'm in the process of getting a clean setup of `auto-sklearn` running on CentOS, and I'm getting an error building the wheel. I know there's quite a few issues about this already, but I've found the cause for at least one of the errors, which is the `./include/rfr/trees/binary_fanova_tree.hpp:329: Error: Syntax error in input(3)`. It has been encountered by other users before, see, e.g., https://github.com/automl/random_forest_run/issues/21, https://github.com/automl/auto-sklearn/issues/314, https://github.com/automl/random_forest_run/issues/18.

It appears to happen in older versions of SWIG, which don't have all support required for C++11 and later versions. Either way, the error is as follows (solution can be found below):

```
running install
running build_ext
building '_regression' extension
swigging pyrfr/regression.i to pyrfr/regression_wrap.cpp
swig -python -c++ -I${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/include -I./include -o pyrfr/regression_wrap.cpp pyrfr/regression.i
./include/rfr/trees/binary_fanova_tree.hpp:329: Error: Syntax error in input(3).
error: command 'swig' failed with exit status 1
```

As the message suggests, this is a problem with line 329 of https://github.com/automl/random_forest_run/blob/v0.6.0/include/rfr/trees/binary_fanova_tree.hpp#L329. Apparently, SWIG has some trouble handling the `>>&`: changing it to `> >&` solves the problem.

issue_body:
## Rollover indices

By rollover indices we mean a group of indices and an alias that relies on the
`_rollover` API to operate. We can assume that each group has a single alias
`PREFIX` (for example `"prod-apache-logs"`) and a series of indices that follow the naming pattern of
`PREFIX-\d{6}` (`"prod-apache-logs-000001"` - `"prod-apache-logs-000007"`). The 
alias points to the last index in the series, this index
(`"prod-apache-logs-000007"`) is the index that is still being written to, we
call that the `active` index.

### Lifecycle

In various points in an index lifecycle we might want to perform operation on
either `active` or `inactive` indices, for example:

* snapshot all `active` indices every hour
* force merge indices that are no longer `active`

We also need to address indices that belong to a certain time period. Since a
rollover index can belong to multiple time periods (as rollover is often
size-based and not time-based) we need to identifyy possibly all of them to be
able to: 

* snapshot all indices containing January data into `archive-201701` repository


## Proposed enhancements

- [ ] add `intersect` value to `stats_result` parameter of the `period` filter
which will identify those indices where `min_value <= range_to AND max_value >=
range_from` (the `period` of the filter and of the index data intersect) (#1045)

- [ ] add `pattern` parameter to `count` filter that would allow it to operate
within groups: given indices `[logs-1, logs-2, logs-3, metrics-1, metrics-2]`
the filter `{filtertype: count, count: 1, pattern: '^(.*)-\d$', reverse: true}` will
produce `[logs-3, metrics-2]` - a list composed of `active` indices for
every prefix. (#1044)
